jito.selfDealingNotes: No token purchase required. JTO governance token exists but does not affect delegation. MEV commission required but performance-based. Top 400 validators selected per 10-epoch cycle via hierarchical scoring.
jito.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, public API, Jito Foundation governance. JIP-25 expanded validator set to 400 and added anti-sybil measures.
jito.mevPolicy: Jito client required for all validators. Tips redistributed to stakers minus commission. MEV commission capped at 10%. Automatic unstaking for delinquency or commission spikes.

doublezero.selfDealingNotes: 5% validator fee REMOVED as of epoch 939. Validators now earn from DoubleZero: 1% baseline (IBRL connection), 2% shred distribution (multicast), up to 4% geographic decentralization bonus. Multicast connection requires one CLI command. 2Z token exists but not required for delegation. Previously pay-to-play, now pay-to-participate (validators are compensated).
doublezero.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, weekly rebalancing. Co-founded by Austin Federa (former Solana Foundation). DZDP Phase II expanded pool from 3M to 13M SOL. Geographic diversity bonus actively incentivizes decentralization.
doublezero.mevPolicy: No specific MEV/Jito requirement. Multicast shred distribution equalizes block data access across validators regardless of stake size.

blazestake.selfDealingNotes: 80% of delegation is algorithmic (no token needed). However, BLZE gauge system controls 10% of delegation and validators can purchase votes via Votex marketplace to direct stake to themselves with positive ROI. This constitutes a partial token flywheel. Protocol buys BLZE with revenues. Gauge percentage planned to increase over time.
blazestake.transparencyNotes: Open governance via SolBlaze DAO, published criteria, community voting. Delegation strategy well-documented at stake-docs.solblaze.org.
blazestake.mevPolicy: Jito client usage tracked. MEV tips flow to stakers. Validators with 50K+ SOL must run 0% MEV commission.

jpool.selfDealingNotes: No token purchase required. Top 400 by APY over last 10 epochs, ranked by JPool score. Top 200 by rank included. Commission <10% on both inflation and MEV. Community Good validators get bonus scoring.
jpool.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, scoring methodology available. Max stake 750K SOL cap. Automatic exclusion for commission spikes.
jpool.mevPolicy: Encourages Jito client. MEV tips redistributed. Commission monitoring in place. bloXroute SWQoS integration for high-yield option.

phase.selfDealingNotes: No token or self-delegation requirement. Simply prioritizes small, independent validators and ecosystem contributors. Commission ≤5%, performance within 3% of cluster average.
phase.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, open application, clear mission. Weekly application review.
phase.mevPolicy: Jito client recommended but not required. Focuses on small validators — MEV tips pass through where available.

vault.selfDealingNotes: Multi-layered extractive flywheel. Validators must: (1) pay 25% of earnings from undirected stake via SaaS invoices, (2) buy $V tokens and lock as veV for gauge influence, (3) self-stake through vSOL/Kamino with up to 7.5x leverage to qualify for Direct Stake Leaders allocation, (4) get opaque Validator Board approval. USDC bribes can influence gauge votes. Failure to pay invoices within 10 epochs = blacklisting.
vault.transparencyNotes: Discord-only application, opaque board approval. Votex.so governance. Most extractive model in the ecosystem.
vault.mevPolicy: No public MEV redistribution policy. Token flywheel prioritizes $V holders over staker MEV returns.

dynosol.selfDealingNotes: No token requirement. Reserves large portion of pool for new and small validators. Requires 100+ epochs consistent activity, 99%+ uptime. Values open-source tooling and community contributions.
dynosol.transparencyNotes: Open validator application, monthly rebalancing, docs available at docs.dynosol.io. SVD (Stake Validator Diversity) scoring.
dynosol.mevPolicy: No formal MEV redistribution policy documented. Validators may run Jito independently.

jagpool.selfDealingNotes: No token flywheel. Regional diversity mission (LATAM, Singapore, South Africa). Validators need SFDP or 40K+ SOL, regional presence, active public profile, and clean track record. Bonus stake for ecosystem contributions.
jagpool.transparencyNotes: Published delegation strategy at docs.jagpool.xyz. 5% rewards fee reinvested for decentralization. xLabs partnership.
jagpool.mevPolicy: Jito client required. MEV tips flow to stakers. Penalizes malicious MEV (sandwich attacks).

stke.selfDealingNotes: Purely algorithmic via StakeWiz Wiz Score (65th iteration). No application, KYC, or token purchase required. Targets 40-60 validators with equal delegation. Operated by SOL Strategies (NASDAQ: STKE), which also operates Laine, Cogent, and Orangefin validators.
stke.transparencyNotes: Algorithmic selection based on 15 metrics and ~130K data points per validator. SOC2 Type II compliant. Note: SOL Strategies owns both StakeWiz (scoring platform) and validators that benefit from delegation.
stke.mevPolicy: No explicit public MEV redistribution policy. Selection based on Wiz Score performance metrics.

marinade.selfDealingNotes: SAM (Stake Auction Marketplace) — validators bid CPMPE (SOL per 1,000 SOL per epoch) for delegation, creating pay-for-stake dynamics. PSR bond required (~1 SOL per 2,000 SOL delegated, slashable for underperformance). MNDE directed stake lets veMNDE holders influence validator stake caps and earn fees (MIP-5). Historical 37K SOL exploit via bid manipulation over 126 epochs.
marinade.transparencyNotes: Open source delegation strategy. SAM auction is well-documented and algorithmic. MNDE governance via Realms. 50% of protocol fees go to MNDE buybacks since Sep 2025.
marinade.mevPolicy: Jito client strongly preferred. Validators internalize MEV earnings into their SAM bids. Tips flow to stakers through mSOL APY. Commission ≤7% required.

shinobi.selfDealingNotes: No token requirement. Pure performance-based selection using on-chain metrics (latency, CU, consensus voting over 10 epochs). Explicitly anti-pay-to-play. Founder (Zantetsu) operates BLADE validator in the pool. Manual blacklist maintained by founder with appeals process.
shinobi.transparencyNotes: All metrics are on-chain and verifiable via stakeview.app. Every-epoch rebalancing. Single-operator governance (Zantetsu).
shinobi.mevPolicy: Jito client required. Performance-based selection, not commission-focused. MEV tips flow to stakers.

edgevana.selfDealingNotes: Validators MUST use Edgevana bare-metal infrastructure to receive delegation. Infrastructure requirement creates direct revenue flywheel — Edgevana profits from both hosting fees and pool management. Algorithm: 50% performance (vote credits, 10-epoch avg) + 10% published info + 10% operating history + 10% Edgevana region weighting.
edgevana.transparencyNotes: Delegation algorithm published at docs.stake.edgevana.com. 1% rewards fee (lowest among multi-validator pools). 5 epochs notice before validator removal. Closed ecosystem — only paying customers eligible.
edgevana.mevPolicy: Jito available on Edgevana infrastructure. Commission-adjusted MEV performance tracked. Algorithm rewards validators who distribute more MEV to stakers.

definity.selfDealingNotes: No token flywheel. APAC-focused geographic delegation strategy. Uses Sanctum Multi program.
definity.transparencyNotes: Published criteria, geographic diversity focus (Asia-Pacific region). Delegation every epoch with blacklist for malicious behavior.
definity.mevPolicy: Jito client encouraged. Geographic diversity focused delegation. Recommended 5% staking / 10% MEV commission.

indiesol.selfDealingNotes: No token requirement. Layer33 cooperative of ~25 independent validators focused on decentralization. Fees fund collective operations and public goods. Members independently operated across global locations.
indiesol.transparencyNotes: Layer33 validator dashboard at validators.layer33.com. Presented at Breakpoint 2025. Active community presence. Controls ~4.14% of total Solana network stake across members.
indiesol.mevPolicy: No formal MEV redistribution policy. Individual validators may run Jito independently. Partners with bloXroute and Astralane for SWQoS routing.

sharkpool.selfDealingNotes: Education-focused pool delegating to university-partnered validators (Princeton, UPenn, others). SharkLabs funds and trains student teams. Limited transparency on delegation criteria. All 10 validators in a single datacenter.
sharkpool.transparencyNotes: Dashboard at app.sharkpool.org. Limited formal documentation — no whitepaper, no public delegation algorithm. SHARK token exists on pump.fun but unclear if official governance token.
sharkpool.mevPolicy: No explicit MEV/Jito policy documented. University validators may run Jito independently.
